question:
Over $30M committed to the Makina public sale?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if the total USDC commitments for the Makina raise on Legion exceed the number specified in the title before the raise closes. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is the USDC-denominated commitments recorded in the allocation contract at:
https://etherscan.io/address/0xd90537959dfb699d9ca4898082b70fb238c3dc1f

If the final USDC commitment amount cannot be verified by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, or if relevant information becomes permanently unavailable during the market period, this market will resolve to “No.”

evidences.title: Makina Project Profile & Token Activities | DropsTab
evidences.content: ## How are vesting and unlocks structured? Priority ICO participants unlock 25% of their tokens at TGE, with the remaining 75% vesting linearly over nine months. Public sale participants on Legion receive 100% at TGE. The design aims to prevent steep sell-offs while maintaining early liquidity for contributors. No large unlock cliffs have been announced. ## Who funded Makina, and how much was raised? Makina raised $3.0M USD in a strategic round on June 25, 2025, at a $35M FDV. Investors include Bodhi Ventures, Interop, and cyber Fund on the venture side, alongside angels such as Aleksander Larsen, Ryan Zurrer, Cozomo de Medici, Ivangbi, Adrian Brink, Dan Reecer, and Robinson Burkey. The same pricing applies to the upcoming IDO on Legion. [...] ## What happened in Season 0 and Season 1? Season 0 ran from September 29 to end of October 2025, bootstrapping liquidity and distributing Points and Tickets for priority ICO access. Users could hold or deposit Machine Tokens on Curve to earn up to 2.4× multipliers through weekly boosts. Season 1 then activated live strategies run by Dialectic, shifting from testing to full operation. ## Is MAK trading now? Where will it list? As of Novemb...

evidences.content: With strong backing from leading Web3 investors, builders, and ecosystem partners, Makina (MAK) is shaping the future of decentralized asset management while enabling participants to engage directly with its expanding cross-chain network. More than just a token, MAK serves as the core governance and utility asset that powers Machines, execution engines, and yield-driven strategies across the protocol—cementing its long-term role in Web3 infrastructure. evidences.title: How to participate in the Legion token sale
evidences.content: Legion uses the mechanics of a preliminary application for participation in the token sale among users who have registered and passed KYC on the platform. After the application is submitted, it is reviewed by the protocol team. The amount of the allocation depends on the Legion Score. Its redemption does not require a quick response, as the FCFS format is not provided. However, depending on the project, whitelist rounds may take place. Detailed information about the sale can be found here. ## Registration on the platform 1. Go to the Legion website and register using your email address or Google account: Protocol home page. Data: Legion 1. Enter the invitation code incrypted and continue. Start registration and enter the code. Data: Legion [...] ## Key points supported networks: Ethereum or Arbitrum; KYC: mandatory; restrictions: incompatible regulatory and legal requirements (not specified); format: submission of applications; participation conditions: Legion Score over 350; allocation calculation: depending on the Legion Score; refund possibility: no. If you have any questions, you can ask them in our X. > All information is provided for informational purposes only and should...

Audited by Enigma Dark, Sigma Prime, and ChainSecurity, Makina’s flagship vaults already protect tens of millions in live capital. More Machines = more TVL = more value captured by $MAK through staking, buybacks, and treasury revenue. The future of programmable asset [...] and treasury revenue. evidences.title: Kraken to host token sales through Legion on its new Launch platform
evidences.content: Discover the institutional crypto exchange LMAX Digital through high-level info and live data here. ✕ Institutional crypto intel Explore platform Legion to host token sales on its new Kraken Launch platform, with Legion aiming to reach a wider user base through the tie-up. Legion, backed by investors including Kraken, will now share select “highest-profile” token sales with the exchange, the firms said Thursday. Tokens from these joint sales will be listed for trading on Kraken shortly after they conclude. [...] Legion’s platform uses a merit-based scoring system, allowing founders to allocate tokens to loyal community members and project-aligned investors. The platform says its token sales are MiCA-compliant in Europe and that it has been in discussions with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission crypto task force as it looks to expand access to U.S. participants as early as Q4. While access to Legion-specific sales will be determined by its scoring system, Kraken Launch offerings will initially open on a first-come, first-served basis. Up to 20% of each sale will be reserved for Legion Score holders, with the rest available publicly on both Kraken and Legion, the firms sa...

evidences.title: WoW Legion Sales Numbers Stacked Up Against Past Launches | The Ancient Gaming Noob
evidences.content: # The Ancient Gaming Noob ## Veni, Vidi, Scripsi The number we did get was 3.3 million copies sold in the first 24 hours. (Yes, they said “more than 3.3 million” in the press release, but if it had been 3.4 million they would have run with that number, so it is likely 3.3 million and change.) As usual, with any number standing alone, I start looking for context. Fortunately, the internet provides context if you look for it. The first day sales track record looks like this: WoW Legion – 3.3 million Warlords of Draenor – 3.3 million Mists of Pandaria – 2.7 million (first week) Cataclysm – 3.3 million Wrath of the Lich King – 2.8 million Burning Crusade – 2.4 million World of Warcraft – 240,000 [...] Lets assume 90% of all active players buy right away. If we also assume that the release brings back some inactive players (lets just say its 1m accounts), that suggests WoW ‘only’ has 2.4ish million active accounts (and even fewer players) before an expansion release. But, that would also mean that for WotLK and such that was also the case, which doesn’t add up with historically reported subscriber numbers. LikeLike 7. Wilhelm Arcturus's avatarWilhelm Arcturus Post author [...] Then t...

evidences.title: Comparable Sales | Fannie Mae
evidences.content: Browse the Guide [...] #### Age of the Comparable Sales Comparable sales that have closed within the last 12 months should be used in the appraisal; however, the best and most appropriate comparable sales may not always be the most recent sales. For example, it may be appropriate for the appraiser to use a nine month old sale with a time adjustment rather than a one month old sale that requires multiple adjustments. An older sale may be more appropriate in situations when market conditions have affected the availability of recent sales, and the changing market conditions causing their use must be explained in the report. [...] When choosing comparable sales, the appraiser should examine the market area of the subject property, assess its characteristics, and identify similar comparable sales. Market area is defined as the geographic region, for a subject property, from which most demand comes and in which most of the competition is located. This does not mean comparable sales must be identical to the subject property, but instead should be competitive and appeal to the same market participants that would also consider purchasing the subject property. If the available comparable...

evidences.content: Giabardo and O'Connor declined to disclose their startup's valuation. The round was for equity and token warrants, or promised allocations of a yet-to-be-launched cryptocurrency, they said. ## ICO boomerang Legion's capital injection comes as ICOs are back in vogue in crypto. In June, the stablecoin project Plasma raised $500 million in five minutes through a public token sale. In July, Pump.fun, a memecoin factory that's become one of the most profitable projects in crypto, raised $600 million in 12 minutes. And Cobie, a pseudonymous crypto influencer and investor, has launched his own ICO platform called Sonar. [...] The two eventually crossed paths and decided to work together to bring back ICOs. Their platform is built to comply with recently released regulatory framework in the European Union for early token launches, they said. The statutes require projects to release a "white paper" that requires issuers to detail, for example, risks involved with the offering and update their white papers anytime there are material changes to the projects. The two cofounders have also met with the SEC to discuss what disclosures the agency should require for public sales, said O'Connor....

evidences.content: Makina (MAK) is the governance and utility token for Makina’s programmable DeFi execution engine on Ethereum. The snippet describes planned public ICO details including a November 25–December 2, 2025 sale window, a $1.30 million target raise, a $0.075 public sale price, 17,333,333 MAK for public sale, and vesting terms of 25% at TGE with the remainder vesting linearly over 9 months.

evidences.title: Makina Foundation / makina-contracts competition | Cantina
evidences.content: Makina is a protocol for executing advanced cross-chain investment strategies. It provides infrastructure for operators to issue tokenized strategies with DeFi composability and risk controls. Each strategy centers on a Machine contract on the Hub Chain for deposits, withdrawals, share pricing, and cross-chain coordination, while Calibers execute strategies across the Hub and supported Spoke Chains. Strategies are defined by a Mandate describing objectives, risk profile, and operating parameters.
